extraction.one_line_summary: WELLENS Tim won from breakaway after 60km formation battle, using corner positioning with 100m to go to block faster finisher WOODS.
tactical_patterns: Breakaway formation required 60km indicating peloton willing to race hard even mid-Vuelta when not mountain stage.
WELLENS pre-studied final 200m twisty corners, positioned front at final left-hander to neutralize WOODS' superior speed.
GC contenders allowed 7-rider strong break because depth into Vuelta made teams unwilling to control flat stages.

rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: positive: executed corner-based positioning strategy to overcome speed deficit against WOODS on uphill finish
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: negative: had superior finishing speed but lost tactically by not controlling final left-hand corner with 100m remaining
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: neutral: attempted late acceleration on punchy finish but suggests awareness his climbing style doesn't suit steep punchy gradients
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: negative: commentary notes steep punchy finish 'not his wheelhouse' indicating vulnerability on explosive gradients versus sustained climbs
